title: teucrium pumilum subsp. carolipaui
intro: Teucrium pumilum subsp. carolipaui, also known as the Pyrenean germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium pumilum plant, which is a type of herbaceous perennial plant belonging to the mint family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Teucrium pumilum subsp. carolipaui include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared for consumption as a tea, tincture, or infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
